Summary: XP not increasing when using two Mending items – one in main hand, one in offhand
Description:
When holding two items with the Mending enchantment — one in the main hand (e.g. sword) and another in the offhand (e.g. pickaxe or shield) — XP stops increasing completely.



Even if both items are at full durability and should not receive XP, the game seems to still prioritize XP repair logic and "gets stuck".



The XP does not go to the Mending items (because they’re full) nor to the player’s XP bar. It seems the game assigns the XP to both tools and never lets go.



⚠️ Important notes:

- The bug only triggers if the item used to **attack (like a sword)** has Mending.

- After this, even removing the items doesn’t fix XP gain — it stays broken until you remove **all Mending items** from both hands and inventory.



🎯 Reproduction Steps:

1. Get two tools with Mending (e.g. sword and pickaxe).

2. Hold sword in main hand and pickaxe in offhand.

3. Kill mobs or collect XP orbs.

4. XP does not go to player’s XP bar.

5. Remove tools — XP still bugged unless all Mending items are gone.



🧠 Theory:

The game prioritizes XP to both items. If both are full, it locks XP in a repair loop and blocks player progression.



This was tested in:

- Minecraft Java Edition 1.21

- Vanilla (no mods)

- Survival and Creative

- New and old worlds



Please fix this — XP is a vital mechanic and this bug can make worlds feel broken or corrupted.

Summary: Some entities freeze upon entering this chunk
Description:
When some entities touch this chunk, they freeze instantly and stop receiving damage from effects like fire. Despite this, these entities can be damaged by the player, but just one first time; after that, they stop taking damage.

Projectiles and thrown items are affected too. Tamed horses don’t freeze just if you are on them, but you can’t dismount them while being in the chunk.

If a mob is killed by explosions from another chunk, the body remains on the floor, not ending the death animation, but turning the mob red and throwing the mob drop. This was tested with a creeper explosion.

Specific cases are, for example:

* A zombie burning down doesn’t die, and it doesn’t start burning if it entered at night.


* A witch keeps the potion effect that was taking when entering the chunk.


* Endermen can’t teleport; in those cases, the particles are still moving.


* Arrows don’t do damage to the player.


* A tamed horse stops freezing if it’s mounted.


* Items can’t be taken and start popping/falling infinitely.


* Shulkers can’t move or open, but their projectiles still move in a straight line and despair at a range.


* TNT makes the explosion animation but just disappears without making any damage.



We were playing on a server. The seed where we found it was `-9101802231045030160`, at XYZ: `166`/`100`/`-270`. When all players exit the world, the bug disappears, making all effects play together.

Summary: Ender Dragon bring about unusual acceleration
Description:
When the Ender Dragon is lingering over the return portal, it has no hitbox except for its head. This allows the player to be positioned inside the dragon's center. At the moment when the Ender Dragon tries to take off again, its hitbox reappears, and the player gets launched away. If the player shoots an arrow at that precise moment, the resulting arrow velocity becomes extremely high—not because the actual knockback on the Ender Dragon is particularly strong, but possibly because knockback is still being calculated internally (server-side?). Since arrow velocity is apparently calculated based on the internal values, the closer the player is to the dragon's center, the more abnormal the resulting arrow speed becomes. This is what is being claimed.

Expected result: Arrow velocity should correspond to the actual knockback experienced by the player.Actual result: Either the internal knockback calculation on the Ender Dragon is absurdly exaggerated, or arrow velocity is being calculated based on internal values—at least one of these must be true, resulting in extremely high arrow speeds.

While it's questionable whether knockback behavior should follow a purely exponential function—especially considering the changing hitbox—it remains unclear how much of this behavior is intentional.

I don't have the equipment or specs to record a video myself, so I can't demonstrate the behavior in the latest version. However, there is an older version video that explains a bug caused by the same underlying factors, which I believe serves as a helpful reference. It should be reproducible using the same steps.

Summary: Game randomly crashes in Singleplayer because of HashMap ConcurrentModificationException
Description:
When attempting to play the game in singleplayer, there's a random chance the game might crash. Most of the time, it's HashMap#computeIfAbsent throwing ConcurrentModificationException.This issue happens because the hash map is the same object in both client and server in singleplayer. This creates a problem where the game can crash because when client thread runs computeIfAbsent on the map, server might add or remove entry from the map and vice versa.

This issue happens pretty sparsely and rarely because in the key is same in both threads most of the time which the exception isn’t thrown if an existing value gets changed when computeIfAbsent is running. This leaves pretty small time gap which makes it hard to reproduce. Though you can create a random string array, use them as keys and put them to HashMap, clear and redo it every tick in a method that runs in both threads to recreate it more easily.

As specified in JDK-8071667, this only happens if you use compute, computeIfAbsent, computeIfPresent or merge methods. It is specific to HashMap and other HashMap types.

In attachments there’s a method that recreates the crash which runs every player tick in both threads. strings is an array of 1000 random strings, map is String to String HashMap.

Summary: 'weathering_copper_trap_door' block type ID is inconsistent with 'trapdoor' block type ID and block names
Description:
Generally, block type IDs seem to be accurate to their respective blocks' names; however, `weathering_copper_trap_door` is one case where this is not true. The block type registry is exposed in the blocks' data generator report, meaning this behavior is observable with the vanilla Minecraft server JAR. 

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Run the data generator


2. Search for `weathering_copper_trap_door` in the output, observing that it exists


3. Search for `trapdoor` in the output, observing that it also exists



**Expected result**

The block type ID shown would be `weathering_copper_trapdoor`.

**Actual result**

The block type ID shown is `weathering_copper_trap_door`.



The output can also be viewed here:

Summary: Baby Happy Ghast does not scale harness layer correctly
Description:
When equipping harness for adult Happy Ghast, then using command to set their age to -24000.

After that the harness layer disappeared and cannot be seen from the outside. But when looking inside the Baby Happy Ghast there is a harness layer rendered but incorrect scale.

I have attached fix code to make it render properly. Just simply remove `baby` argument and remove `apply` part with the `BABY_TRANSFORMER` in the `HappyGhastHarnessModel` class.

Summary: Item sorting broken with Copper golem in underground warehouse setup.
Description:
In Snapshot 25w32a, I encountered a consistent issue with the Copper Golem's item sorting behavior. The golem fails to deposit items into chests that already contain the same item, even when those chests are not full.

For example, I placed diamonds into a regular chest. Later, I added more diamonds into a Copper Chest. Instead of depositing them into the original chest (which still had space and already contained diamonds), the golem chose a different chest — sometimes even one containing unrelated items like copper.

My storage system includes two separate underground warehouses, each with 10 chests (5 on the left, 5 on the right). Each warehouse has its own Copper Golem, so there's no overload. All chests are on the same level, with plenty of space above them. The only visual indicator I used was a single item frame on one chest, but the golem ignored it.

The issue occurs every time I attempt to sort items. The golem seems to forget which chests it has already used and does not consistently check all 10 available chests. This behavior makes the sorting system unreliable and breaks the intended functionality.

Please investigate this bug — it significantly affects gameplay and automation.

Summary: Copper golems refuse to open chests being viewed by a player in Spectator mode
Description:
Steps to reproduce:

1. Set up any environment where a copper golem would open a copper chest.


2. Enter spectator mode and click on the copper chest to view it’s contents.



Expected Behavior:

1. In the circumstance that a player is in spectator mode, players should not be able to make any noticable changes to the world.


2. The copper golem should be able to interact with the copper chest as if no player is currently opening it.



What happens:

1. The copper golem will refuse to open the copper chest, allowing players in spectator mode to break or interrupt contraptions that use copper golems.



This bug makes it annoying to debug and analyze the speed of copper golem contraptions when using spectator mode.

Summary: Possible regression: GuiGraphics#submitOutline doesn't respect matrix transformations
Description:
GuiGraphics#renderOutline has been removed in 25w33a in favor of GuiGraphics#submitOutline.

Any transformations which have been applied to the Matrix3x2fStack (such as translations) are not respected anymore.



Previous example which used to work:

```java
guiGraphics.pose().translate(10, 10);
guiGraphics.renderOutline(0, 0, 5, 5, 0xFFFFFFFF);
```

The above would render a white outline at `10, 10` with width and height each being 5 in white color.



Current state which doesn’t work:

```java
guiGraphics.pose().translate(10, 10);
guiGraphics.submitOutline(0, 0, 5, 5, 0xFFFFFFFF);
```

This outline now doesn’t render at `10, 10` but at `0, 0`.



Expected behavior would probably be for it to render at `10, 10` as was previously the case. The fill command also respects applied transformations, so it would be weird for `submitOutline` not to.

Summary: Intel core related bugs still occur on servers with resource/texture packs enabled.
Description:
**Bugs -**  and probably MC-296908 still occur on 1.21.8 if server has resource packs enabled. 

Sometimes textures flicker with black lines or their animations are interrupted. This is easily noticed on magma blocks, lava, and water.

Game screen freezing and crashes also occur as before.

## How to get

Join java multiplayer server with resource/texture pack enabled on device with intel core graphic, wait a while and you should notice glitches. Sometimes, spotting them can be less frequent and seems to depend on the server.

## Note:

These bugs didn't seem to exist on version 1.21.8 for a while, but they've been around for a few days or weeks (I can't quite pinpoint it). They don't seem to occur on servers without resource/texture packs enabled or in singleplayer worlds.Also the bugs seem to be much weaker than on versions before intel core graphic fixes

Summary: Game freezes graphically after ~20 seconds
Description:
This bug has affected multiple versions, since around April.

Launching the game ends in the window freezing after 20~30 seconds without fail, regardless of whether it’s in the main menu or being played. The freeze is only graphical, though. The game can still technically be played {presumably; movement sounds play when pressing the movement keys, and the mouse cursor appears when pressing the pause or inventory keys} despite the view being frozen.

This was tested on multiple clients (including the official Launcher,) before and after both a Java Runtime and graphics driver update, and before and after deleting the .minecraft folder.

Summary: Carpet disappears when placed above transparent wall piston door upon activation
Description:
When using a transparent wall piston door with Redstone, carpets placed above the moving blocks disappear upon activation.The carpet does not drop as an item, but simply gets deleted.

This issue does not affect every identical build – some piston doors work fine with carpets, others consistently delete them when triggered.



Steps to reproduce

Build a transparent wall piston door (hidden door using sticky pistons and Redstone).

Place carpets on top of the blocks that are part of the piston mechanism.

Activate the Redstone circuit (lever, button, or any power source).

Observe: When the pistons extend or retract, the carpet vanishes.



Expected result

The carpet should remain placed on top of the block even when the piston door is activated.



Actual result

The carpet disappears completely.

It does not drop as an item.

Happens only on certain piston door builds, making it inconsistent.



Additional Notes

Issue only occurs with carpets, not with full blocks.

Seems related to block updates when pistons move blocks beneath carpets.

Tested on Minecraft Java 1.21.8 – may affect other 1.21.x versions as well.
